title: DAF Modernization Process Model
text:
Acquisition Process Model v14.6
The CPI Team of SAF/AQXP updated the DAF Acquisition Process Model to version 14.6. This version updates several of the AFLCMC Standard Process Documents and adds a new feature to the landing pages. For a complete list of changes, refer to the Document Changes page where you can now find the new feature of a link to the document that’s been changed. These links will only be available for the most recent major release of the APM. If you have any recommendations, questions, or concerns, please contact the CPI Team through our Contact Us page.
Continue Reading
Acquisition Process Model v14.3
The Acquisition Process Model was update to version 14.3 by the SAF/CPI Team. This version of the model includes the addition of the Guide to Research Other Transactions and the updated version of the AFLCMC Standard Process for Contract Data Management. For a full list of changes to any version of the model, please refer to the Document Changes page. If you have any suggestions, comments, or questions, please go to the Contact Us page.
Continue Reading
Acquisition Process Model 14.2
The SAF/AQX CPI Team has published version 14.2 of the DAF Acquisition Process Model. This version of the model has updates to the DoD OTA Guide, AFLCMC Standard Process Guide for Logisitics Health Assesssment, AFLCMC Standard Process for Contract Award (Source Selection), and DAFI 38-401 Continuous Process Improvement. For a full list of document changes, please refer to the Document Changes page. If you have any questions, comment, or suggestions please reach out to us using the Contact Us page.
Continue Reading
Acquisition Process Model version 14.1
The SAF/AQX CPI Team has updated the Acquisition Process Model to version 14.1. This version includes new content based on DAFI 63-101/20-101, updates based on revised A5/7 Guidebooks, the inclusion of Digital Engineering as a specialized view, and updates to several templates. For a full list of all the documents added/changed in this version of the APM, please refer to the Document Changes page. If you have any questions, comments, or changes please reach out to us using the Contact Us form.
Continue Reading
